source_feedback: Maintainer-curated verdicts for the refreshed Sentrux review packet. The ranked sample set now dedupes same-source duplicates, keeps explicit propagation obligations and task-presentation boundary findings ahead of clone followthrough debt, and treats missing-test findings as supporting watchpoints rather than primary actions.

verdicts.engineer_note: This explicit propagation obligation is the clearest high-leverage finding in the packet. When the defect injection toolchain changes without the remaining required surfaces, the agent should see this before generic watchpoints or secondary followthrough debt.
verdicts.expected_v2_behavior: Keep this on the lead surface as a direct propagation gap with concrete repair sites and verification guidance.

verdicts.engineer_note: This is the strongest contract-surface finding for task_presentation_status. A direct forbidden raw read should stay ahead of the related import-bypass and coverage watchpoints on the same concept.
verdicts.expected_v2_behavior: Surface this as the primary task_presentation_status fix and keep it above the adjacent bypass and coverage debt.

verdicts.engineer_note: This bypass is real boundary drift, but the direct forbidden_raw_read on the same concept is the higher-value lead action. Keep the bypass as supporting context rather than the headline.
verdicts.expected_v2_behavior: Keep this visible as secondary task_presentation_status context behind the direct raw-read finding.

verdicts.engineer_note: This session introduced a new clone across src/copy.ts and src/source.ts. It is a concrete regression and should stay ahead of generic test-coverage reminders.
verdicts.expected_v2_behavior: Keep this on the primary supporting surface as direct clone debt with an obvious repair path.

verdicts.engineer_note: This is a concrete clone followthrough miss in the changed buildAccessUrl path. It should stay above generic watchpoints because it names the missed sibling fix directly.
verdicts.expected_v2_behavior: Keep this visible near the top of the supporting surface as a concrete followthrough target.

verdicts.engineer_note: This is a second concrete clone followthrough miss in the same family. Keep it behind the primary buildAccessUrl drift but ahead of generic test reminders.
verdicts.expected_v2_behavior: Keep this as supporting clone followthrough debt, not as background noise.

verdicts.engineer_note: src/app/task-dashboard.ts is missing direct sibling coverage. Keep missing_test_coverage visible as supporting regrowth pressure, but not ahead of the direct propagation, boundary, or clone findings in this packet.
verdicts.expected_v2_behavior: Keep this in the supporting watchpoint section behind the primary propagation, boundary, and clone surfaces.
